config.systemPrompt:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

methodology: One read-only external-project analysis task executed through a DeepSeek LLM agent; it is operationally measured but not success-scored.
with_caesar: LLM has access to 18 CAESAR graph tools (including find_dead_code) plus read_file.
without_caesar: LLM has access to read_file + grep only.
Fresh conversation per task x condition x run. No shared history or cache between conditions.
Paid microbenchmark protocol: exact deepseek-v4-flash, temperature 0, fixed per-round seeds, identical prompts/order/limits/runtime; no model or provider fallback.
Mode: live API calls. Suite: external-project.
Cost is estimated from token usage and configured pricing. Wall time includes API latency.
ROI claims blocked when baseline invalid or sample size too small.

evidence: Graph-verified zero incoming CALLS, USAGE, READS, and TESTS edges for all listed symbols via find_dead_code (confidence: high).
Spot-checked source confirms SecuritySection is exported via `export function SecuritySection` at src/app/admin/sections/SecuritySection.tsx:119 but has no importers.
Spot-checked source confirms ProposalEditorModal is exported via `export default function ProposalEditorModal` at src/app/admin/components/ProposalEditorModal.tsx:122 but has no importers.

AdminMailingPage at src/app/admin/mailing/page.tsx:3527 is a 4556-line page component with zero references across the graph.
NodeConfigModal at src/app/admin/escenarios/NodeConfigModal.tsx:1986 is a 1110-line modal component with zero references.
PromptsInventoryClient at src/app/admin/prompts/PromptsInventoryClient.tsx:101 is a 1310-line client component with zero references.
Additional 24 candidates also verified (total 30) — see full find_dead_code output.
result.summary: Found 30 high-confidence dead-code candidates (functions/components) with zero incoming graph references. The top 6 are SecuritySection, ProposalEditorModal, LiveView, AdminMailingPage, NodeConfigModal, and PromptsInventoryClient — all defined and exported but never imported or called anywhere. These are ideal removal candidates for a NEW_WEBSITE cleanup sprint. Note: some are exported (export function/export default) which means they could be public API surface consumed externally or dynamically — verify with your team before deleting.

evidence: SidebarSearch is exported from src/app/admin/components/sidebar/SidebarSearch.tsx but never imported anywhere. The actual sidebar (Sidebar.tsx) imports SidebarLogo, SidebarNavSection, SidebarFooter, and SidebarOrgChartFlyout — but not SidebarSearch.

The ui-utils.tsx file in the mailing module exports 6 components (SkeletonLoader, EmptyState, LoadingOverlay, ProgressBar, Toast, HoverCard) but only CampaignStatusIcon and SectionAppleIcon are actually imported from that file (in StatusBadge.tsx, MailingNavigation.tsx, and page.tsx).
StatusBadge.tsx in the mailing components exports 4 components (StatusBadge, StatusBadgeWithTooltip, CampaignProgress, MetricCard) that have zero import references anywhere in the codebase.
MailingBreadcrumb is exported from MailingNavigation.tsx but never imported anywhere — only MailingNavigation itself is used.

OpsAppleBadge is exported from OpsAppleIcon.tsx alongside OpsAppleIcon, getOpsAppleIconKindFromText, and getOpsAppleIconKindForAgent — all three of the latter are used in OperationsSection.tsx and CommunicationsSection.tsx, but OpsAppleBadge has zero import references.
result.summary: Found 13 exported functions across 4 files that are defined but have zero callers or import references anywhere in the codebase. The most impactful cleanup targets are: the 6 unused UI utility components in src/app/admin/mailing/ui-utils.tsx (SkeletonLoader, EmptyState, LoadingOverlay, ProgressBar, Toast, HoverCard), the 4 unused status components in src/app/admin/mailing/components/StatusBadge.tsx (StatusBadge, StatusBadgeWithTooltip, CampaignProgress, MetricCard), SidebarSearch in src/app/admin/components/sidebar/SidebarSearch.tsx, MailingBreadcrumb in src/app/admin/mailing/MailingNavigation.tsx, and OpsAppleBadge in src/app/admin/components/OpsAppleIcon.tsx. These appear to be leftovers from prior development iterations that were never wired into the active UI.

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "MATCH (n:Function) WHERE n.qualified_name CONTAINS 'OperationsSection' AND n.qualified_name ENDS WITH 'OperationsSection' RETURN n.name, n.file_path, n.qualified_name, size(n.incoming_calls) as inc_calls, size(n.incoming_usages) as inc_usages, size(n.incoming_reads) as inc_reads LIMIT 5"}

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "MATCH (n:Function) WHERE n.qualified_name CONTAINS 'AdminMailingPage' AND n.qualified_name ENDS WITH 'AdminMailingPage' RETURN n.name, n.file_path, n.qualified_name, size(n.incoming_calls) as inc_calls, size(n.incoming_usages) as inc_usages, size(n.incoming_reads) as inc_reads LIMIT 5"}

evidence: Graph-based dead-code analysis across CALLS, USAGE, READS, and TESTS edges found zero incoming references for all reported symbols.
trace_path inbound check on OperationsSection, AdminMailingPage, and AnalyticsSection confirmed zero callers.
All symbols are non-exported (is_exported: 0) and definition_verified: true with 'high' confidence.
The find_dead_code tool scanned 30+ candidates across admin sections, components, pages, scenarios, and API routes.
result.summary: Found 30+ unused symbols (functions & classes) in the codebase with zero callers, zero usages, zero reads, and zero tests. The top 10 above range from ~400 to 11,000+ lines each, concentrated in src/app/admin/ sections, components, and pages. The largest candidates are OperationsSection (11,049 lines), AdminMailingPage (4,556 lines), and OpsQwenConfigPanel (1,902 lines) — representing significant cleanup candidates for the NEW_WEBSITE sprint.

evidence: File src/app/admin/mailing/ui-utils.tsx exports 8 functions: SkeletonLoader, EmptyState, LoadingOverlay, ProgressBar, Toast, HoverCard, CampaignStatusIcon, SectionAppleIcon.
Only CampaignStatusIcon and SectionAppleIcon are imported anywhere (by page.tsx, MailingNavigation.tsx, and components/StatusBadge.tsx via 'from ./ui-utils' or 'from ../ui-utils').

The other 6 exported functions have zero import references across all .ts/.tsx files in the project (confirmed by searching for 'import.*SkeletonLoader', 'import.*EmptyState', etc. which all returned no matches).
A separate local EmptyState exists in AuditLogSection.tsx (non-exported) but the exported one from ui-utils.tsx is never consumed.
These functions appear to be dead code — written as utility components but never wired into any importing consumer.
result.summary: Found 5+ unused exported functions in src/app/admin/mailing/ui-utils.tsx. The file exports 8 helper components total, but only CampaignStatusIcon and SectionAppleIcon are actually imported by other modules (mailing page.tsx, MailingNavigation.tsx, StatusBadge.tsx). The remaining 6 exports — SkeletonLoader, EmptyState, LoadingOverlay, ProgressBar, Toast, and HoverCard — are defined with full implementations (props interfaces, JSX rendering) yet have zero callers across the entire source tree. Note: there is also a 6th unused export (HoverCard) in the same file. These are safe candidates for removal.

comparison.interpretation: Estimated from token usage and published pricing
Correctness of task results vs expected

summary.sample_size_note: Based on 0 deterministic measured runs with real API calls (3 evaluated including partial assertions).
summary.roi_blocked_reason: Deterministic sample size too small (0 runs). Need at least 6.

warnings: External project mode: read-only source at /Users/admin/Desktop/MENTESIA/NEW_WEBSITE; CAESAR state remains temporary.
note: external_dead_code en NEW_WEBSITE: la diferencia mas brutal registrada hasta ahora. CAESAR 100% success (3/3), baseline 0% success (0/3). El baseline sin CAESAR es un desastre: 299s y 52 tool calls de media vs 21s y 11 de CAESAR. Sin grafo, el modelo hace grep masivo (45-57 greps por run) y lee cientos de KB de archivos intentando adivinar que funciones no tienen callers — y falla estrepitosamente: los 3 runs del baseline reportaron falsos positivos (simbolos que SI tenian referencias). CAESAR con find_dead_code consulta directamente el grafo (CALLS + USAGE + READS + TESTS edges) y devuelve resultados precisos en 4-14 tool calls. Coste: -98% ($0.002 vs $0.105 mediana). Wall time: -93% (21s vs 299s). 0 bytes leidos con CAESAR vs hasta 1.4MB del baseline. El caso es tan extremo porque encontrar codigo muerto sin grafo es intratable: requeriria leer cada archivo y hacer grep de cada simbolo, O(N*M) en tamaño de codebase. Con grafo es O(1): los nodos con in_degree=0 ya estan precalculados. Esta es LA tarea donde CAESAR muestra su mayor ventaja competitiva.
